key_insight: Lean can be used not just to verify theorem-proving outputs, but to provide dense, step-level reward signals that improve reinforcement learning for formal proof search.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical training recipe for proof assistants and LLM theorem provers that uses symbolic verification to deliver more informative credit assignment than binary success/failure rewards.
method: Parse proof attempts into tactic sequences and use Lean elaboration to identify locally sound steps and the first failing step.
Train with a GRPO-style RL objective augmented by first-error propagation and first-token credit for process-level feedback.
Combine outcome-level verification with tactic-level supervision to balance final proof correctness and intermediate proof quality.
evidence_signal: Experiments on STP-Lean and DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5 show tactic-level supervision outperforming outcome-only baselines in most settings, with gains reported on MiniF2F and ProofNet.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable tactic parsing and Lean’s elaboration behavior, so integration complexity and overhead may limit portability across proof environments.
paper_title: Process-Verified Reinforcement Learning for Theorem Proving via Lean
